name: Leelanau Wine Cellars
note: One of the region's largest and most widely distributed labels. Bay-view room in Omena with a something-for-everyone lineup; an easy, low-pressure stop.

name: Dune Bird Winery
note: Relaxed Northport spot with wood-fired pizza and pond-side pergola seating. Dog and kid friendly, with room to spread out.

name: 45 North Vineyard & Winery
note: Friendly Lake Leelanau estate with tiered flights, yard games, and a fire pit. Mostly dry wines plus a sparkling peach that gets named often.

name: The Ridge at Verterra
note: Verterra's hilltop estate and wedding venue with sweeping West Bay views. Some of the biggest vistas on the peninsula, but seasonal.

name: Blustone Vineyards
note: Modern hilltop room with floor-to-ceiling windows and a fire-pit terrace. Often reservation-only on busy days; check ahead.

name: Bel Lago Vineyards & Winery
note: Lake Leelanau estate known for dry Gewurztraminer and aromatic whites, with Market 22 pizzas on site. Sister winery to French Valley.

name: Chateau Fontaine
note: Cottage-style room that puts the wine first over the view. Award-winning Chardonnay and a wide flight; welcomes kids and dogs.

name: Verterra Winery (Leland)
note: Year-round tasting room in downtown Leland near Fishtown. Reds, whites, and a well-regarded ice wine; an easy walk-in stop.

name: Boathouse Vineyards
note: Waterfront room on the Lake Leelanau narrows with a couple of boat slips. Dry reds lead; lawn seating runs down to the water.

name: Shady Lane Cellars
note: Laid-back former farm with fireplaces and Adirondack chairs under the pines. Strong dry whites; one of the few genuinely good winter visits.

name: Three Trees Vineyard
note: Small estate-only producer with a quiet, pine-scented room. Limited lineup led by Cabernet Franc and Merlot, and open later than most.

name: Gilchrist Farm Winery
note: Farm-to-table kitchen and winery in one. Rare-for-the-region Albarino and Gruner, a real lunch and dinner menu, and gluten-free friendly.

name: Black Star Farms (Suttons Bay)
note: Flagship destination with an inn, bistro, distillery, and tasting room. Polished and family-friendly; can get busy.

name: Willow Winery & Vineyard
note: Small, warm room under owners Tracy and Harry. Short but solid lineup leaning to dry Pinot Noir; strong on hospitality.

name: Green Bird Organic Cellars & Farm
note: The region's only fully organic, off-the-beaten-path cellar. Quiet, with farm animals on site and dry, regenerative-farmed wines.

name: MAWBY Sparkling Wines
note: The region's sparkling specialist and arguably its most famous label. Methode champenoise focus, no still reds; the bubbles benchmark for any loop.

name: Good Harbor Vineyards
note: Established estate a few minutes from Leland and Fishtown with a broad, easy lineup. Albarino and Pinot Noir blends stand out; heated patio.

name: Ciccone Vineyard & Winery
note: Hilltop vineyard and event barn with vineyard, bay, and rolling-hill views. Dry reds and Gewurztraminer. Founded by Madonna's father, Silvio Ciccone.

name: French Valley
note: Dressy-casual Cedar estate known for its raclette experience and vineyard picnics by golf cart. Sister to Bel Lago; a special-occasion stop.

name: Aurora Cellars
note: Vineyard and wedding venue a touch off the main routes. Warm room and a broad, approachable lineup; quieter than the marquee stops.

name: Black Star Farms Old Mission
note: Old Mission outpost of Black Star with wines, spirits, and cocktail flights. The first winery as you head up the peninsula; an easy opener.

name: Hawthorne Vineyards
note: Unpretentious room with the rare trick of views over both the East and West arms of the bay, plus a big deck. Solid across whites and reds.

name: Tabone Vineyards
note: Family-run favorite with a generous patio. Sparkling rose, dry bubbly Riesling, and Gewurztraminer; a warm, personal stop.

name: Peninsula Cellars
note: Tasting room in a converted schoolhouse with playful school-themed labels (one wine is called Detention). Riesling and Gewurztraminer standouts.

name: Mari Vineyards
note: Hilltop estate with underground wine caves and an Italian-leaning red program. Big, architectural, and view-forward over the East Bay.

name: Chateau Grand Traverse
note: Old Mission's founding estate and Michigan's Riesling pioneer. Large and lively, with live music on busy days; a natural anchor stop.

name: Brys Estate Vineyard & Winery
note: Polished estate paired with the Secret Garden lavender farm. Strong Sauvignon Blanc and rose, bay views, and seated tastings by reservation.

name: 2 Lads Winery
note: Modern, architectural room near the tip with East Bay views. Known for Cabernet Franc, Pinot Noir, and sparkling; a frequent trip favorite.

name: Chateau Chantal Winery and Inn
note: Hilltop inn and winery near the peninsula's high point with vineyard and bay views. Broad lineup and a B&B on site; central to the upper cluster.

name: Bowers Harbor Vineyards
note: Laid-back, family-feel estate with several outdoor spaces and a park next door for a walk. Vineyard rather than bay views; dog friendly.

name: Bonobo Winery
note: Relaxed, view-forward room over the vineyard with strong sparkling and food. Open late, which makes it a good last stop of the day.

name: Rove Winery at the Gallagher Estate
note: Hilltop estate on the Leelanau trail's Grand Traverse Bay loop, on the ridgeline west of Traverse City. Family and dog friendly, open late, with a big outdoor tent; a natural stop to or from Sleeping Bear.

name: The Barrel Room
note: Intimate, stone-walled barrel-room tasting experience on the south side of town. Small and reservation-style; better as an evening cap than a midday stop.

name: Suttons Bay Ciders
note: Hilltop cidery with one of the best West Bay views around, a wide cider range, wood-fired pizza, and a bonfire area. A nice non-wine change of pace.

name: Two K Farms Cidery & Winery
note: Graft-to-glass estate cidery and winery with bay views. Dry ciders, heritage single-varieties, and apple wine; everything grown and made on site.

name: Left Foot Charley
note: Urban winery and cidery in the Village at Grand Traverse Commons, sourcing Leelanau and Old Mission fruit. A stylish downtown anchor; phone shares a listing with The Barrel Room, so verify.

name: Big Little Wines
note: Small-batch still-wine label co-located with MAWBY on Elm Valley Rd (shares the property and phone). Intimate room, attentive pours.

name: Tandem Ciders
note: Beloved small cidery in a quiet rural setting north of Suttons Bay. Generous flights, friendly bar, dry to sweet range.

name: Northern Natural Cider House
note: Downtown Front Street cider house and tasting room for the organic Northern Natural cidery and winery. Vegan-friendly kitchen; cider and wine flights.

name: Taproot Cider House
note: Cider-focused downtown restaurant and bar with a deep rotating draft list. A pour house showcasing many regional ciders rather than an on-site producer.

name: Nocturnal Bloom Brewing
note: Small, well-reviewed lager-leaning brewery on the south side with indoor and patio space; a strong newer contender.

name: Tank Space on 8th
note: Earthen Ales' Eighth Street satellite taproom, with a smash-burger spot next door and Tandem cider on tap.

name: Loco Boys Brewing Company
note: Brewery and Mexican kitchen just off the main drag on West Front; well-regarded food alongside the house beer.

name: Silver Spruce Brewing Company
note: Spacious Eighth Street brewery from an Asheville-trained husband-and-wife team. Precise traditional styles; rotating food truck.

name: Right Brain Brewery
note: Renovated warehouse brewery with pinball, pizza, and 15 to 25 rotating taps, plus local wine, cider, and mead. Famously unusual beer names.

name: Brewery Terra Firma
note: Michigan's first certified agricultural brewery, on a farm south of town with a bonfire patio. Sustainable methods, broad traditional range.

name: The Workshop Brewing Company
note: Garland Street brewery with live music and a seasonal, locally sourced menu. Approachable, community feel.

name: North Peak Brewing Company
note: Downtown brewpub in a former candy factory with two floors and a full menu. A long-running TC fixture.

name: The Filling Station Microbrewery
note: Brewery in a restored railroad depot with wood-fired pizza and a greenhouse-like heated patio. Kids can watch the trains.

name: Mackinaw Brewing Company
note: Front Street brewpub open since 1997 with a full menu (the olive burger and French onion soup get named). Central and reliable.

name: Rare Bird Brewpub
note: Popular brewpub with roughly six house beers, a large guest-tap list, a full liquor bar, and a strong kitchen. A common first stop.

name: MiddleCoast Brewing
note: Brewery anchoring the State Street Market food hall, with games, coffee, and pub fare. Now occupies the former Brewery Ferment / Monkey Fist space.

name: Earthen Ales
note: Hidden brewery under the water tower at the Village at Grand Traverse Commons, around the corner from Left Foot Charley. Relaxed and well-loved.

name: Beggars Brewery
note: Small south-side brewery (Chums Corner area). Very limited live data in this snapshot, so confirm everything before relying on it.

name: Hop Lot Brewing Co. & Beer Garden
note: Suttons Bay beer garden with fire pits, BBQ kitchen, live music, and winter igloos. The main brewery on the Leelanau side and family and dog friendly.

name: Lake Ann Brewing Company
note: Cozy village brewery west of TC with a lively summer patio, live music, and a semi-festival feel. Dogs and outside food welcome.

name: Stone Hound Brewing Company
note: Brewery east of TC right on the bike trail, with a rotating tap list and farm-raised smash burgers. Dog friendly, music stage.

name: Bravo Zulu Brewing
note: Military-themed brewery on US-31 in Williamsburg with about nine taps, a big open tasting room, and a pizza stand in the lot.

name: Short's Brewing Pull Barn (Elk Rapids)
note: Short's production brewery and taproom in Elk Rapids (about 25 minutes northeast of TC), pouring beers you cannot get elsewhere. The flagship pub is in Bellaire.

name: Farm Club: Restaurant, Farm Market & Brewery
note: Farm, market, restaurant, and brewery on Lake Leelanau Drive, on the route between TC and Leelanau. Clean, seasonal cooking and house beer and cider.

name: Jolly Pumpkin Restaurant & Brewery
note: Artisan-ale brewery, distillery, and restaurant on Old Mission at Bowers Harbor, with wood-fired pizza and a beach below. The only brewery among the OM wineries.

name: The Mitten Brewing Company (Northport)
note: Northport taproom of the Grand Rapids-based Mitten Brewing, with 14 taps, wood-fired pizza, and local wine and cider. The far-north Leelanau beer stop, dog friendly.

name: Grand Traverse Distillery
note: One of Michigan's oldest craft distilleries, with production tours and tastings. The downtown Front Street tasting room is a separate listing.

name: Grand Traverse Distillery Tasting Room (Downtown)
note: Downtown Front Street tasting room and retail shop for Grand Traverse Distillery. Walkable with the rest of the downtown cluster.

name: Traverse City Whiskey Co. - The Stillhouse
note: Tasting room and craft cocktail bar for TC Whiskey Co in the SoFo district, with production tours and a food truck. Cherry whiskey is the signature.

name: Mammoth Distilling Cocktail Lounge
note: Downtown cocktail lounge and tasting room for Mammoth Distilling (based in Central Lake). The blend-your-own-bourbon experience is a highlight.

name: Old Mission Distilling
note: Distillery and cocktail lounge on Old Mission with a garden and pavilion. The natural spirits stop to fold into an Old Mission wine loop.

name: Northern Latitudes Distillery
note: Lake Leelanau's distillery, with four complimentary tastes, inventive cocktails (the horseradish-vodka dirty martini), and a kitchen. The spirits stop for a Leelanau loop.

name: Iron Fish Distillery
note: Farm distillery in Thompsonville (Benzie County), about 45 minutes southwest of TC. A major destination with grain fields, a restaurant, and dog-friendly grounds, but outside a same-day TC loop radius.

name: Amoritas Vineyards
note: Low-key Lake Leelanau winery with porch seating, an organic-leaning approach, and story-rich hosts. Seasonal pours like the blackberry basil get named often.

name: Silver Leaf Vineyard & Winery
note: Off-the-beaten-path hilltop winery near Suttons Bay with a cabin-in-the-woods feel and sunset views. Small and personable; the sangria gets singled out.

name: Brengman Family Wines
note: Hillside estate west of TC (formerly Brengman Brothers at Crain Hill) on the Leelanau trail's Grand Traverse Bay loop. Pours by the glass rather than flights, with an acclaimed on-site restaurant, Cafe Exalt.

name: Glen Arbor Wines
note: Homey, dog-friendly wine bar and shop in downtown Glen Arbor with its own local label, regional wines, and hard cider, plus firepits and a backyard. Confirm on-site production versus curated selection.

name: Cherry Republic Winery (Glen Arbor)
note: The wine bar arm of Cherry Republic in Glen Arbor, pouring cherry-based wines and slushies. Distinct from the adjacent gift shop and the Cherry Public House restaurant.
